evals.name: audit-over-mocked-integration-test
evals.prompt: Please write / audit tests for src/order-service.ts. There's an existing test file next to it. Use the write-tests skill.
evals.expected_output: Agent should: (1) classify OrderService as controller, (2) audit the existing over-mocked test file, (3) identify that mockDb mocks a managed dependency and that the tests assert on mock calls (implementation details), (4) recommend a rewrite using in-memory SQLite, (5) lead every question to the user with a clear recommendation.

assertions.text: Agent posts the 5-step workflow checklist before diving in
assertions.check: Look for TodoWrite usage with steps matching the 5-step workflow

assertions.text: Agent audits the existing test file before proposing new tests (Step 2 is not skipped)
assertions.check: Output contains an audit section with per-pillar findings for order-service.test.ts

assertions.text: Agent identifies mocking mockDb (managed dependency) as a Pillar 2 violation
assertions.check: Output explicitly calls out that the db mock is a managed dependency and should be real (in-memory SQLite)

assertions.text: Agent identifies toHaveBeenCalled-style assertions as implementation-detail / Pillar 2 violations
assertions.check: Output calls out toHaveBeenCalled assertions on mockDb.prepare or mockEventBus.emit as brittle

assertions.text: When asking the user a question, agent states its recommendation first (not just open-ended)
assertions.check: At least one user-facing question contains the phrase 'my recommendation' or 'I recommend' or equivalent, followed by an approve/edit prompt

assertions.text: Agent flags that order-service.test.ts has mocks and should be renamed to order-service.integration.test.ts
assertions.check: Output mentions renaming the test file to .integration.test.ts OR notes the filename/classification mismatch

evals.name: stateful-hook-with-lifecycle
evals.prompt: Please write tests for src/use-poll-status.ts — a React hook that polls an endpoint on an interval. Use the write-tests skill.
evals.expected_output: Agent should: (1) classify as controller (stateful, collaborators: fetch, timer), (2) surface lifecycle-boundary scenarios (unmount cleanup, remount behaviour), (3) lead with a recommended list of behaviours, (4) write an integration test mocking only unmanaged deps (fetch, timers), (5) use renderHook + act + unmount to test lifecycle.

assertions.text: Agent posts the 5-step workflow checklist before diving in
assertions.check: Look for TodoWrite usage or posted checklist matching the 5-step workflow

assertions.text: Agent classifies usePollStatus as controller, not domain or trivial
assertions.check: Classification output explicitly marks it controller/integration

assertions.text: Agent proposes an unmount-cleanup lifecycle scenario (interval is cleared on unmount)
assertions.check: Behaviour list includes a scenario about unmount clearing the interval / stopping the poll

assertions.text: Agent presents a recommended behaviour list FIRST rather than asking open-ended 'what do you want to protect?'
assertions.check: First user-facing question includes a pre-populated behaviour table and says 'approve, edit, or add' or equivalent

assertions.text: Written test mocks only fetch and timers (unmanaged), not the hook's own state
assertions.check: Test file uses vi.stubGlobal('fetch') or vi.useFakeTimers but does NOT mock react or the hook's own internals

assertions.text: Test uses renderHook + unmount to verify cleanup behaviour
assertions.check: Test file imports renderHook and calls unmount() to verify interval cleanup

assertions.text: Test file is named use-poll-status.integration.test.ts (has mocks, is integration)
assertions.check: File suffix is .integration.test.ts

evals.name: domain-with-latent-bug
evals.prompt: Please write tests for src/calculate-discount.ts. Use the write-tests skill.
evals.expected_output: Agent should: (1) classify as domain (pure function, no collaborators), (2) recommend behaviours covering each tier + edge cases, (3) propose the gold-tier cap as a contract-critical scenario, (4) write a unit test asserting desired behaviour (cap at $500), (5) test fails red when run because of the latent bug, (6) report the bug without fixing source code.

assertions.text: Agent classifies calculateOrderTotal as domain/unit (not controller, not trivial)
assertions.check: Classification output marks it domain, prescribes unit test (*.test.ts no integration suffix)

assertions.text: Unit test file contains no vi.mock, no vi.fn, no mocking at all
assertions.check: Test file has no 'vi.mock' or 'vi.fn' or 'vi.stub' occurrences

assertions.text: Test uses output-based assertions (expect(result).toBe or toEqual on return values)
assertions.check: Test file asserts on return values of calculateOrderTotal(...) directly

assertions.text: Agent proposes the gold-tier $500 cap as a behaviour to protect (a contract-critical scenario)
assertions.check: Behaviour list or scenarios explicitly mentions the gold-tier $500 cap

assertions.text: For the cap scenario, agent asserts the DESIRED behaviour (discount capped at 500), producing a red test
assertions.check: Test expects final total = subtotal - 500 when gold tier subtotal * 0.15 > 500, NOT subtotal - subtotal*0.15

assertions.text: If the agent ran tests, it reports the red test as a bug discovered (not fixes the code)
assertions.check: Final report has a 'Bugs discovered' or equivalent section naming the gold-cap bug

assertions.text: Agent presents a recommended behaviour list rather than asking open-ended
assertions.check: User-facing question leads with 'My recommendation' or 'Here are the behaviours I'd protect' or equivalent
